sections.paragraph:
-

(1) i.q. Arab. قَدَّ, قَذَّ to cleave (kindred roots גָּדַד, גָּזַז, and the like; see גָּדַד . To this agree Gr. κεδάω, κεδάζω, σκεδάζω). Hence קִדָּה cassia, and קָדְקֹד top.

(2) denom. from קָדְקֹד; to bow down; hence to incline oneself out of honour and reverence. [This meaning is made a separate root in Thes.] Always in the fut. in the Chaldee form יִקֹּד, וַיִּקֹּד; plur. וַיִּקְּדוּ. Often followed by הִשְׁתַּחֲוָה, which is stronger; Gen 24:26, וַיִּקֹּד הָאִישׁ וַיִּשְׁתַּחוּ לַיהֹוָה “and the man bowed down and prostrated himself before Jehovah;” Exo 12:27 34:8 Num 22:31 1Ki 1:16. Elsewhere קָדַד in a wider sense, includes both. 1Sa 24:9, וַיִּקֹּד דָּוִד אַפַּיִם אַרְצָה 1Sa 28:14 1Ki 1:31.
